factors.name: Nvidia earnings & AI momentum
factors.summary: Stocks soared on Nvidia earnings with strong AI demand signals; SoftBank surged ~20% on OpenAI and SB Energy IPO plans, indicating sustained hyperscaler capex confidence.

factors.name: Samsung strike resolution
factors.summary: Samsung reached tentative union deal, eliminating strike risk at world's largest memory chipmaker; KOSPI futures surged 5%, sidecar triggered, signaling semiconductor supply chain relief.

factors.name: Japan export strength
factors.summary: Japan exports beat 14.8% y/y vs 9.3% forecast in April; machinery orders strong 5.9% y/y; massive trade surplus of 301.9bn yen supports tech manufacturing demand.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: BoJ contemplating June rate hike with inflation near 2% target; ECB June hike nearly certain per Reuters sources; US inflation data (CPI, PCE) not yet released, limiting conviction on near-term Fed pivot.

factors.name: Treasury yields & real rates
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y Treasury or TIPS data in release window; DXY hovering ~99.0 on mixed US-Iran peace talk signals, offering no clear directional cue for duration-sensitive Nasdaq.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran, Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Trump signaled 'final stages' Iran negotiations but threatened restart of attacks if terms rejected; Strait of Hormuz closure ongoing, oil at $98+ WTI, risk-off backdrop; safe-haven flows evident in yen, gold ~$4,540.

factors.name: China capital flows & yuan
factors.summary: Global funds piled back into Chinese stocks in April post-Iran selloff; PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8349 (yuan strength), injected 100bn yuan via repos; suggests reopening appetite for Asian growth.

factors.name: Australia labor weakness
factors.summary: Australia unemployment jumped to 4.5% vs 4.3% expected, highest since Nov 2021; employment fell 18,600; flash PMI slumped to 47.8 (contraction), denting global growth narrative.

factors.name: Valuation / Multiple compression risk
factors.summary: Jamie Dimon warning on bond market rout and inflation risk; private credit risks flagged by Australian regulator; junk spreads near two-decade lows amid complacency; late-cycle froth evident.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO filing reveals 18,712 BTC holdings (7th largest public holder) and $billions in AI capex; Intuit cutting 17% workforce on AI threat (short-term pain, longer-term efficiency story); OpenAI IPO plans drive SoftBank rally.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is grinding higher on sustained AI/semiconductor tailwinds (Nvidia, Samsung strike resolution, strong Japan exports) and renewed China capital inflows, offsetting geopolitical uncertainty (Iran tensions, Strait of Hormuz closure) and early signs of global growth softening (Australia PMI/jobs weak). The 4h +0.32% and 24h +0.65% moves are modest and in early/mid-phase, suggesting conviction is building but not yet extended. However, major US inflation data (CPI, PCE due Friday 21 May 2026 at ~1930 ET per Japan CPI schedule notes) remains a wildcard that could reprices Fed expectations and trigger a sharp rotation if hotter than expected.
catalysts_ahead: Japan April CPI (May 21, 2026 ~2330 GMT / 1930 US ET) — currently forecast 1.7% core y/y vs 2% BoJ target; if surprise to upside, supports BoJ June hike narrative, potential yen strength headwind to USD-heavy Nasdaq revenue
US CPI / Core CPI (early week likely, Friday May 23 or earlier) — critical for Fed re-pricing; hot print = multiple compression, bearish long-duration tech
FOMC decision (June timeframe implied, not imminent) — but forward guidance and dot-plot signal key to duration valuation
US-Iran peace deal outcome (next 24–72h possible, Trump says 'final stages') — resolution bullish equities/oil, escalation risk-off
ECB June 11 rate hike (nearly certain per Reuters sources) — may support EUR, increase comparative appeal of USD yield
Mega-cap earnings continue (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META updates; NVDA already beat) — each can move NQ by 2–3%
screening.risk_warning: If US inflation prints hotter than expected (CPI >3.5% core, PCE >3.0% core), market reprices Fed terminal rate higher, real yields spike, and long-duration tech (NQ overweight) suffers sharp compression. Conversely, if Iran deal fails and Strait of Hormuz threat escalates, oil shock could break risk-off, driving flight to safety and rotating NDX lower. Current phase is in-flight after overnight earnings beat; if intraday pullback toward 29100–29200 occurs, next move is uncertain pending CPI/PCE release.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE 2 (modest gains underway post-earnings, not yet extended); await retest of 29250–29350 before adding longs, or wait for CPI release (Friday likely) to establish clearer Fed narrative before increasing NDX exposure. Risk-reward improves on any dip toward 29100 support if macro data remains benign.

factors.name: Nvidia AI momentum & price target raise
factors.summary: Melius raised Nvidia stock price target to $400 on TAM growth; Nvidia earnings signal strong AI momentum driving SoftBank shares up 20%. Key hyperscaler capex story intact despite geopolitical headwinds.

factors.name: US-Iran peace talks (Trump final stages)
factors.summary: Trump statement on 'final stages' of Iran talks triggered risk-on rotation Wednesday. Oil price compression (WTI ~$98) and geopolitical de-risking support equity sentiment, though still fragile.

factors.name: European economic contraction & demand weakness
factors.summary: France PMI collapsed to 43.5 (5.5-year low), Germany PMI 48.6 (contraction second month). Middle East war hitting consumer demand and margin pressure across services; EasyJet CEO notes caution on summer bookings.

factors.name: Treasury yields rebounding on inflation risks
factors.summary: 10-year yields resuming climb as traders monitor inflation risks from oil shock and wage pressures. Real yields critical for long-duration growth (NQ exposure); higher real yields compress multiples.

factors.name: Asia-Pacific mixed signals: Japan strong, India/Australia weak
factors.summary: Japan April exports +14.8% beat (semiconductor shipments soar), Nikkei +3.1%, but Australia unemployment jumped to 4.5% (highest since Nov 2021), complicating RBA easing outlook. Mixed earnings backdrop.

factors.name: Oil supply shock & energy cost margins
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs: global oil stockpiles falling at record 8.7M bbl/day as Hormuz flows at 5% of normal. JPMorgan warns India earnings face downside from elevated energy costs and margin pressure.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings resilience (SoftBank, Generali beats)
factors.summary: SoftBank +20% on AI momentum, Generali +2% on Q1 beat with FY27 guidance confirmed. Earnings resilience in pockets supports NDX despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: Dollar strength on geopolitical uncertainty & hawkish Fed bets
factors.summary: USD maintaining strength (DXY ~99.10) on Iran uncertainty and hawkish central bank messaging (ECB Rehn signals potential June hike, BoJ Koeda warns inflation already ~2%). Strong dollar pressures NQ international revenue.

factors.name: VIX/volatility and positioning
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news window; markets described as 'optimistically positioned' by analysts but facing chop into macro uncertainty. Dealer gamma and options expiries not highlighted as extreme.

factors.name: Tech tariff & antitrust risks (implicit geopolitical tail)
factors.summary: No direct antitrust news today, but Taiwan uncertainty lingers; AMD $10B Taiwan investment and Nvidia ceding China AI market to Huawei underscore export control & geopolitical fragmentation risk for semis.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in a push-pull between AI earnings strength (Nvidia TAM, SoftBank rally, megacap resilience) and macro headwinds (European demand collapse, energy cost shock, hawkish central banks, real yield rebound). Yesterday's Trump Iran peace comment triggered a risk-on bounce (+0.32% 4h, +0.65% 24h), but the move is now entering Phase 2 consolidation as investors digest Europe's worsening PMI data and oil supply disruption's margin impact. Treasury yields are rising again on inflation fears, pressuring long-duration growth multiples that dominate the NQ. Prior bias (BUY 6/10 from May 21 03:32) has not seen confirmation; current setup is more cautious.
catalysts_ahead: US inflation data (CPI, Core CPI, PCE) — timing TBD but critical for yield repricing
FOMC meetings and Fed speaker schedule (Barkin, Powell comments on inflation/growth trade-off)
Mega-cap earnings calendar: MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN updates on cloud capex and guidance
US–Iran peace deal outcome or escalation (Trump-linked binary risk)
ECB June policy meeting (Rehn hints at possible hike if adverse scenario confirmed)
Trade data and tariff announcements (China tensions, DXY direction)
screening.risk_warning: Bias breaks SELL if: (1) Iran talks collapse or escalate → oil shock, margin compression accelerates; (2) CPI/PCE surprise hot → real yields spike, 10-year breaks 4.5%, multiple compression hits NDX hard; (3) European contagion spreads to US corporate earnings misses; (4) Fed signals faster hikes than priced, DXY rallies further, NQ international revenue headwinds worsen. Conversely, moves break BUY if peace deal confirms + Europe stabilizes + earnings guides up.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (post-Trump bounce, consolidation underway). Wait for retest of 29150–29200 support with volume confirmation before adding longs; or take resistance at 29400+ only on break with fresh positive catalyst (e.g. cooler inflation, Iran deal signed, or NVDA/MSFT beat with raised guidance). Avoid chasing rallies into macro uncertainty and yield rebound headwinds.

factors.name: Nvidia earnings & AI capex signal
factors.summary: Nvidia posted 85% YoY sales growth with muted share reaction but sparked tech-led rebound across Asia (Nikkei, KOSPI higher). Baird raised NVDA price target on AI infrastructure growth; hyperscaler capex remains robust driver.

factors.name: Quantum computing government support
factors.summary: US planning $2B quantum awards to 9 firms with equity stakes (IBM establishing foundry with $1B CHIPS funding, Infleqtion securing $100M). Quantum stocks surged premarket but narrow sector relevance to broad NDX.

factors.name: Real yields & long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields wavering amid US–Iran peace deal speculation. Lower nominal yields typically bullish for growth, but inflation expectations (5y5y swaps) rising—real yields remain elevated. Tech multiples remain under pressure from real rate uncertainty.

factors.name: Eurozone & UK economic contraction
factors.summary: UK Composite PMI 48.5 (miss vs 51.6 expected), Eurozone Composite PMI 47.5 (miss vs 48.8), both marking multi-year lows. France hit hardest; oil shock and Middle East conflict weighing on demand. Stagflation fears rippling through industrial metals and equities.

factors.name: Inflation fears intensifying
factors.summary: Bloomberg warns stocks not effective inflation hedge as investors pile in despite rising inflation fears. UK industrial orders falling at quickest pace since 2020; expected selling prices +38 (highest since Feb 2023). BCA Research warns meaningful equity selloff needed to cool bonds.

factors.name: Iran geopolitical premium & oil shock
factors.summary: Iran Supreme Leader orders near-weapons-grade uranium stay in-country; oil supplies through Strait of Hormuz 95% below normal. Oil prices higher; energy-dependent sectors struggling. Uncertainty on US–Iran deal delays risk-on momentum.

factors.name: Microsoft stock weakness & sentiment drag
factors.summary: Microsoft's sliding stock identified as S&P 500's biggest drag. AI woes and valuations under pressure. MSFT is 10%+ weight in NDX; single-name weakness can drag broad index 1-2%.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings momentum
factors.summary: Nvidia beat; Eli Lilly positive obesity trial data; SoftBank +20% on AI momentum tie-in. Earnings season showing pockets of resilience despite macro headwinds, supporting selective tech support.

factors.name: Hedge fund profit-taking in semiconductors
factors.summary: Reports of hedge funds trimming chip stock positions after rally. Profit-taking in SMH, SOXX suggests late-cycle positioning entering distribution phase.

factors.name: 4h/24h price action already in motion
factors.summary: NDX +0.32% in 4h, +0.65% in 24h—modest gains already posted. Move is underway but not extended; suggests Phase 2 consolidation rather than fresh breakout or reversal setup.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between competing forces: Nvidia's robust earnings and quantum capex optimism on the bullish side, vs. surging inflation expectations, Eurozone/UK economic contraction, Microsoft weakness, and Iran geopolitical risk on the bearish side. Real yields remain elevated despite lower nominal yields; 10y–2y inversion and stagflation fears are suppressing growth multiple re-rating. The index is in Phase 2 consolidation following early-session gains, with hedge fund profit-taking evident in semis. Macro uncertainty (US–Iran peace talks, Fed policy signals, PMI misses overseas) is capping conviction.
catalysts_ahead: US S&P Global Flash PMI (May) — today, afternoon; weak eurozone/UK PMI may set tone
Memorial Day (Friday) — shortened trading week, lower liquidity into close
Earnings: miscellaneous mega-cap pre-bell/post-bell (AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META still ahead in earnings season)
Fed speakers/policy signals — ongoing chatter on rate path and inflation tolerance
Iran–US peace deal developments — intraday headline risk on Strait of Hormuz and energy prices
Treasury yield moves — real rate re-pricing on inflation/Fed expectations
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) US–Iran peace accord announced suddenly, triggering sharp oil crash and risk-on rally (+2-3% NDX on flight-to-growth); (2) Surprise dovish Fed pivot or soft CPI/PCE print driving real yields sharply lower; (3) Major mega-cap (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) beats earnings with bullish guidance, reigniting tech enthusiasm. Conversely, be alert to hot inflation print or hawkish Fed speaker, which would accelerate BEARISH unwind.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias too mixed and Phase 2 consolidation—avoid fresh longs. Consider retest of 29,150–29,200 support before sizing long entry; if 29,450 resistance breaks cleanly on volume AND US PMI beats, reassess to BUY. Short setups off 29,450 if PMI disappoints and real yields spike.

factors.name: NVDA earnings + guidance
factors.summary: NVDA reported strong earnings yesterday but stock failed to rally significantly; now trading -1.86% intraday. Positive fundamentals offset by concerns over China AI chip market ('largely conceded' to Huawei per CEO Jensen Huang), which risks future revenue growth narrative.

factors.name: Semiconductor complex weakness
factors.summary: NVDA down 1.86%, INTC down 3.57% intraday. Broad chip sector weakness despite NVDA beats suggests sector rotation or growth multiple compression concerns dominating individual earnings surprises.

factors.name: Treasury yield surge (Iran geopolitical)
factors.summary: 10Y yields rising sharply amid Iran-Strait of Hormuz tensions and uranium enrichment headlines. Elevated yields compress long-duration growth multiples—directly bearish for high-beta NDX components (mega-cap tech, unprofitable AI names).

factors.name: Oil prices +3%, Hormuz supply crunch
factors.summary: WTI surged >3% after Iran Supreme Leader statements on uranium; physical supplies through Strait 95% below normal. Energy shock raises inflation concerns and real yields, dual headwind for equities. Earlier Iranian denial of strict uranium order briefly eased pressure but geopolitical tail risk remains elevated.

factors.name: US PMI data (mixed manufacturing strength, services soft)
factors.summary: May flash Composite PMI 51.7 (flat vs April 51.7), Manufacturing 55.3 (beat 53.8 exp), Services 50.9 (miss 51.1 exp). S&P Global notes 'damaging economic impact from war in Middle East increasingly evident.' Growth resilient but momentum softening.

factors.name: Intuit earnings crash (-20%+ post-earnings)
factors.summary: INTU plunging >20% after lowering TurboTax revenue outlook and restructuring; fresh AI displacement fears ripple across software/SaaS cohort. Signals potential earnings risk for business model disruption in high-multiple subsectors.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings mixed (NVDA beat, INTU miss, Walmart/Deere beat)
factors.summary: NVDA strong fundamentals but weak follow-through; INTU capitulation; Walmart solid sales growth; Deere beats. Earnings quality scattered—not enough conviction in broad earnings momentum to support rally into headwinds.

factors.name: Risk appetite dimming beneath surface
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes 'signs of trepidation building beneath record highs.' Retail (Walmart outlook worse-than-expected on high gas), consumer discretionary softening. Breadth divergence suggests liquidity and sentiment fragile.

factors.name: Fed rate hike repricing
factors.summary: FOMC minutes showed 'gradual hawkish shift.' Higher US yields now reflecting expectations for multiple rate hikes post-Iran energy shock. Real yields (TIPS) rising, worst for high-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Crypto, AI infrastructure IPO flows (positive micro signal)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO filing with $1.45B Bitcoin holdings; Blockchain.com confidential IPO filing; Quantum computing stocks soaring on $2B government grant program; Bloom Energy +12% on Nebius AI data center deal. Positive narrative overlay but not broad enough to move macro.

factors.name: Yield spread pressure on EUR and GBP
factors.summary: EUR and GBP both down ~0.9% vs USD since mid-May; energy shocks and yield differentials driving FX weakness. Strong USD typically headwind for multinational tech revenue but offset by equity-supportive lower foreign demand outlook.

factors.name: Housing starts decline, jobless claims benign
factors.summary: US jobless claims fell to 209k (solid), but housing starts declined in April amid rate pressure. Mixed labor market backdrop—no recession signal yet but stress in rate-sensitive sectors evident.

factors.name: Bias history: BUY6/10 → NEUTRAL5/10
factors.summary: Prior two reads today were BUY6/10 (03:32, 07:51); now NEUTRAL5/10 (11:22). Recent downgrade from BUY to NEUTRAL reflects deterioration intraday: Iran escalation, yield surge, INTC weakness, INTU crash likely catalysts for the shift. Momentum fading.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 opened this morning with a BUY bias on strong NVDA earnings, but the narrative has deteriorated materially over the past 4–6 hours. Iran geopolitical escalation (uranium enrichment, Hormuz toll discussions) has pushed WTI oil +3%, Treasury yields sharply higher, and triggered a 'risk-off' reassessment. While individual earnings (NVDA, Deere, Walmart) remain mixed-to-positive, the macro backdrop of rising real yields, energy-driven inflation concerns, and broadening sector weakness (semis, software) is overpowering single-name beats. The 10Y yield surge is the dominant headwind—long-duration growth multiples, which comprise ~60% of Nasdaq 100 market cap, are highly sensitive to real rate moves. Meanwhile, INTC's -3.57% drop and INTU's >20% earnings crash signal emerging cracks in the narrative. The market has repriced from 'NVDA rally carries the day' to 'inflation and rates are the real story,' pulling equities lower despite yesterday's strong chip earnings. This is a classic Phase 2 scenario: the initial BUY signal has been challenged by unfolding macro news, and the market is now in a retest/consolidation zone.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX Starship launch (possibly as soon as Thursday) — event-driven headline risk
OPEC+ meeting June 7 on oil output quotas — supply-side energy signal
Fed speakers and FOMC meeting (next ~10 days) — will reveal degree of hawkish repricing
Major tech earnings still pending: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META (likely next 2 weeks)
US CPI/PCE inflation data (mid-to-late June) — will confirm or deny energy shock inflation spillover
Ongoing Iran-US diplomatic developments and Hormuz shipping data — real-time geopolitical risk
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US tensions de-escalate sharply (diplomatic breakthrough) or oil prices retreat >5% intraday, yield compression and risk-on sentiment could rapidly flip the bias back to BUY. Conversely, any further hawkish Fed signal or earnings miss from mega-caps (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) would accelerate the selloff. The 10Y yield level (currently elevated) is the critical fulcrum—break above 4.50% is deeply bearish for NDX; move below 4.20% would be bullish. Watch breadth indicators (advance-decline, put-call ratios) closely: if they deteriorate further, the move down could extend to 19,500 support.
screening.entry_guidance: Nasdaq 100 is in Phase 2—the move down is underway but not yet extended. Retest of 19,600–19,650 support or a pullback to the 50-day MA (~19,750) would offer a lower-risk entry for longs if the macro backdrop stabilizes. For shorts, any bounce to 19,900–20,000 on relief rallies (Hormuz de-escalation headlines) could provide a near-term short entry. Do not chase the selloff here; wait for either support confirmation or a fresh catalyst (e.g., FOMC hawkish surprise) to commit.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Headlines
factors.summary: Reports of finalized draft agreement with Pakistan mediation (FXStreet 17:22, Investinglive 17:19) sparked relief rally. Trump stated 'Iran conflict will end soon' and wants Hormuz 'open, free with no tolls' (Investinglive 16:17). De-escalation would ease energy inflation, supporting equities.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility & Energy Inflation Fears
factors.summary: WTI trading above $101/bbl with conflicting signals on Iran uranium enrichment (Bloomberg 17:12). Secretary Rubio noted tolling system would make deal 'unfeasible' (Investinglive 15:13). Elevated energy prices push 10-year Treasury yield to ~4.6% with 5% in reach (Bloomberg 16:48), compressing high-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations & Inflation Commentary
factors.summary: Fed's Goolsbee called inflation 'pretty significant problem' (Investinglive 15:49); Barkin said 'current policy in good place to respond' but cautious (FXStreet 17:31). Middle East inflation shock threatens to keep rates higher longer, delaying cuts. Bond market showing stress signals (MarketWatch 17:41).

factors.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10Y yield climbing to 4.6% (highest since Iran war started per Bloomberg 16:48) and mortgage rates hit 6.5%+ (MarketWatch 16:04). Long-duration growth/tech stocks extremely sensitive to real rate increases. Yield spike directly compresses NDX valuations.

factors.name: Nvidia Earnings Miss & AI Disappointment
factors.summary: Nvidia posted record $81.6B revenue but stock fell post-earnings (Bloomberg 17:10). BofA called dip 'noise' and raised target, but sentiment reflects AI capex/demand concerns. Semiconductor weakness: NVDA -1.86%, INTC -3.57% (Investinglive 14:46). This is the largest NDX constituent.

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech & Retail Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Tech sector 'experienced noticeable slump,' with chip stocks leading downside (Investinglive 14:46). Walmart issued worse-than-expected guidance citing elevated gas/consumer stress (7% drop, US Top News 16:06). Consumer and tech both showing cracks, core NDX growth engines stalling.

factors.name: Positive AI Infrastructure & Alternative Plays
factors.summary: Spotify surged 15% on AI music deal and guidance (US Top News 17:45). Energy/infrastructure AI basket has outperformed Nvidia 2x (US Top News 17:42). Bloom Energy +12% on Nebius data center deal (US Top News 15:16). Pockets of strength exist outside mega-cap chip names.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Mega-IPO Hype
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO marketing itself as $26.5T AI opportunity play (Bloomberg 17:07), retail access via major brokerages (Finance 15:52). Mega-cap tech IPO pipeline (SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic) suggests underlying risk appetite. However, this is early-stage hype, not immediate driver.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: EUR/USD under pressure from stronger USD (FXStreet 16:18), GBP/USD down on weak UK PMI and USD strength (FXStreet 16:08). Strong USD headwind for NDX (heavy international revenue exposure), but can support stability if geopolitical fears ease.

factors.name: VIX/Risk-On Sentiment Mixed
factors.summary: Stock market at record highs but 'signs of trepidation building beneath surface' (Bloomberg 14:58). Mixed sector rotation (tech down, healthcare steadying, alternatives outperforming). No clear risk-off capitulation or euphoria.

screening.narrative: NAS100 has rallied +1.17% in 24h and +0.98% in 4h on hopes of US-Iran peace deal and de-escalation narrative. However, the market is caught between two conflicting forces: (1) geopolitical relief/Iran deal optimism pushing equities higher, and (2) surging real yields (10Y at 4.6%, mortgage rates 6.5%+) driven by inflation fears from elevated energy/commodity prices. Nvidia's post-earnings stock weakness and broad tech sector slump (Intel -3.57%, semis lagging) underscore fragility in the core driver of NDX gains. Fed commentary (Goolsbee hawkish, Barkin cautious) signals rate-cut hopes are fading into June. The move is in progress (PHASE 2) but faces headwinds from rising real yields and earnings disappointment.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal announcement (expected within hours per Investinglive 17:22)
Treasury yield direction if deal confirmed (likely 10Y pullback to 4.3-4.5% on risk-off energy fears)
Next Fed speaker commentary or inflation data (CPI, PCE) — likely in coming days
FOMC meeting minutes or schedule confirmation
Mega-cap earnings continues (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN if not yet reported)
Oil price stabilization (key for real yield/inflation narrative)
Weekly jobless claims or PMI data (risk-off signal if weak)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal falls apart and oil spikes >$110/bbl (yields rip higher, equities capitulate); (2) CPI/PCE surprise hot again, forcing Fed hawkish pivot and real yields accelerate; (3) Nvidia or other mega-cap earnings guide down capex/AI demand (core narrative collapse); (4) 10Y yield breaks above 4.8% (structural compression of growth multiples beyond absorption); (5) Bank/credit stress signals emerge as rates stay elevated (rout in financials and breadth).
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway with cross-currents (peace hopes vs. real yield surge). Avoid chasing. Wait for either: (a) Iran deal confirmation + 10Y yields pullback below 4.4% for risk-on re-entry at 29350-29500, or (b) retest of support at 29200 if yields hold 4.6%+ for short entry. Confidence capped at 5 due to competing macro forces and extended intraday move.

factors.name: US-Iran geopolitical headlines
factors.summary: Oil volatility from draft US-Iran agreement reports created whipsaw: WTI settled -1.94% to $96.35 after initial spike, uncertainty over ceasefire and Hormuz closure threatens 2008-scale recession per Rapidan Energy. Elevated energy prices risk persistent inflation, forcing Fed to hold rates higher longer.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yields and mortgage rates
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury climbed to ~4.6% with 5% within reach per Bloomberg; 30-year mortgage rates jumped to 6.51% (highest since August), biggest weekly rise since March per Freddie Mac. Long-duration growth multiples under pressure; NDX components especially sensitive.

factors.name: Real yields and inflation expectations
factors.summary: War-driven energy shock re-ignited inflation concerns; Fed's Barkin flagged nervousness about inflation and employment tails. TIPS-implied inflation expectations remain sticky; higher real yields compress NDX valuation multiples despite soft nominal nominal move.

factors.name: Fed messaging: cautious, data-dependent
factors.summary: Fed's Barkin said current policy is 'in a good place' but eschewed forward guidance due to AI labor market uncertainty and ongoing shocks. No hawkish pivot yet, but no dovish signals; wait-and-see tone limits equity support.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings momentum (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT)
factors.summary: NVDA beat with record $81.6B revenue but stock dumped; BofA called it a buying dip but sentiment remains cautious. Hyperscaler capex guidance (AWS, Azure, GCP) not yet refreshed; no fresh bullish catalyst from earnings cycle.

factors.name: AI capex and semiconductor outlook
factors.summary: US announced $2B quantum computing investment across 9 companies; Anthropic-Microsoft AI chip deal in talks; Spotify announced AI music deal with UMG. Infrastructure/energy AI trade doubled money, outpacing Nvidia per MarketWatch. Positive structural AI narrative remains intact.

factors.name: Retail/consumer stress signals
factors.summary: Walmart CFO flagged consumers filling gas tanks less (stress signal); Walmart warned rising fuel costs squeezing margins and could drive higher grocery prices. Iran war oil shock creating cost-of-living headwind for discretionary tech spending.

factors.name: Dollar strength amid geopolitical premium
factors.summary: USD/JPY near 159.30, USD/CAD +0.20% on day as oil retreat and safe-haven flows support DXY. Stronger dollar headwind to NDX international revenue (~40-50% of mega-cap earnings) but supports equity safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Credit market warning signal
factors.summary: MarketWatch reported bond market flipped script: credit diverging from equities with spreads widening, historically credit is 'the one telling the truth.' Risk-off signal buried under equity complacency.

factors.name: Price action: 4h -0.02%, 24h +0.75%
factors.summary: Minimal 4h movement and modest 24h gain suggest market is coiling into geopolitical headlines and yield volatility. No confirmed directional breakout yet; consolidation ahead of Iran deal clarity or next inflation print.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between structural AI capex tailwinds (quantum, Anthropic-MSFT, SpaceX IPO hype) and near-term macro headwinds (US-Iran tensions, oil volatility, rising real yields, mortgage rates at 6.51%). Fed's Barkin struck a cautious, data-dependent tone with no rate guidance, avoiding both hawk and dove signals. The bond market is flashing divergence—credit spreads widening while equities hold—a historically bearish warning. Mega-cap tech earnings beat but disappointed on follow-through; hyperscaler capex commentary missing. Oil whipsaw from failed Iran deal headlines is re-igniting inflation fears and long-duration growth multiple compression.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal announcement or failure (within 24h per last reports)
Oil price reaction to geopolitical resolution or escalation
CPI or PCE inflation print (TBD date; watch for Fed re-pricing)
Next FOMC meeting (date TBD; Barkin comments suggest June hawkish risk)
SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic IPO roadshows/pricing (mega-cap sentiment catalyst)
Hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) earnings guidance and capex updates
Weekly jobless claims / NFP (AI labor displacement emerging risk per Barkin)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal closes and oil falls back below $95, equities could re-rate higher on relief rally and falling real yields. Conversely, Hormuz closure or escalation could spike oil to $110+, forcing Fed to hike and crushing NDX multiples. Credit market divergence (spreads widening) is a canary; if it accelerates into the close, risk-off acceleration likely and bias flips to SELL with 7+ confidence. Mega-cap earnings beat-and-dump pattern suggests narrative fatigue on AI—watch for sentiment rotation into energy/infrastructure names, which would cap NDX upside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation with conflicting signals; avoid chasing until either (1) US-Iran deal clarity collapses oil risk premium and 10Y yields ease back toward 4.3%, OR (2) credit spreads stabilize and hyperscaler capex ramps. Consider tactical long entry on pullback to 29100-29200 support with tight stop (29050) if geopolitical risk abates. Avoid size until yield volatility subsides or Fed extends hawkish hold further out.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings momentum
factors.summary: Workday +14% on AI margin strength; Nvidia beat with $81.6B revenue (BofA: buy dip). Single-name strength in mega-caps provides technical underpinning but comes after recent earnings cycle.

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Mortgage rates hit 6-month highs (6.51%) on inflation fears from Iran war disruption. Long-duration growth stocks face headwinds as real yields remain elevated; 10Y trajectory uncertain amid geopolitical premium.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (US-Iran negotiations)
factors.summary: Draft agreement reportedly finalized (Al-Arabiya), oil down 1.94% to $96.35, easing inflation near-term fears. However, deal still unconfirmed and gaps remain; risk of re-escalation keeps volatility bid and equities vulnerable.

factors.name: Fed policy stance
factors.summary: Fed's Barkin (5/21) says current policy 'in good place to respond' but eschews forward guidance; nervous about labor/inflation tails. No hawkish surprise, but uncertainty prevents dovish relief rally.

factors.name: Inflation and supply disruption fears
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz flow 95% below normal; Rapidan warns Hormuz closure through August risks 2008-scale recession. Walmart warns consumers crimping spending on high fuel costs. Energy/inflation tail risk elevated despite draft Iran deal.

factors.name: AI capex and semiconductor demand
factors.summary: Meta, Broadcom, Applied Materials et al. launching $125M UCLA semiconductor hub; Quantum stocks soaring on $2B US gov grants; Trump delayed AI exec order (supportive of industry). Capex thesis intact but valuation concerns persist (Canada Pension warns on AI-fueled multiples).

factors.name: Valuation and positioning
factors.summary: Canada Pension CEO warns of 'increasingly uncomfortable' AI-inflated tech valuations; BofA notes Nvidia dump despite beat suggests profit-taking. Extended moves in NQ limit upside, margin for disappointment rises.

factors.name: Dollar stability
factors.summary: DXY near 99.20 after strong PMI data; USD stable. No currency tailwind or headwind to NQ international revenue at present.

factors.name: VIX and volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX spike reported in latest news; geopolitical overhang implies elevated implied vol. Equities up only +0.88% in 24h — muted move suggests choppy, directionless consolidation.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating after a modest +0.88% 24h advance, supported by strong mega-cap tech earnings (Workday, Nvidia) and AI capex announcements (Meta/Broadcom chip hub, quantum grants). However, real yields remain elevated on war-driven inflation fears (mortgage rates 6.51%, Strait of Hormuz 95% below normal flow), and valuation concerns mount as long-term investors grow uncomfortable with AI-fueled multiples. The draft US-Iran agreement (if confirmed) could ease oil and inflation fears near-term, but deal remains unconfirmed and gaps persist—keeping volatility and re-escalation risk high. Fed's Barkin offers no relief guidance, maintaining uncertainty.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal announcement (hours, per Al-Arabiya)
Japan CPI (Friday 5/22, expected third consecutive sub-2% core reading)
ECB's Philip Lane speech (Friday 5/22, Singapore AMPF)
FOMC decision/Powell presser (likely next week, date TBD)
Weekly jobless claims, inflation data (PCE due mid-week next week)
Mega-cap earnings continue (guidance commentary critical)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapses or escalates → oil spikes, inflation re-priced higher, yields spike, growth multiples compress sharply. Alternatively, surprisingly dovish Fed guidance or CPI miss could trigger rotation; conversely, hot inflation print would break lower. Extended positioning in mega-cap tech leaves NQ vulnerable to profit-taking on any disappointing earnings revision or macro disappointment. Monitor real yields (TIPS) and 10Y closely—a break above 4.5% would be very bearish for duration-heavy Nasdaq.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 after overnight/early-morning Iran deal chatter; muted +0.88% 24h gain suggests consolidation rather than committed breakout. Wait for retest of 29100–29200 support or clear break above 29500 with volume confirmation before committing; near-term event risk (Iran deal finality, Friday economic data) argues for low conviction entries now.
